subject: Biology
title: What Are Bacteria?
description: A micro-lesson that makes What Are Bacteria? usable.

hook.text: Bacteria are single-celled organisms with no nucleus. They were the first life on Earth.

buildup.text: A typical bacterium is about one micrometer long—a thousand times smaller than a grain of sand.

discovery.text: They come in three main shapes: spheres, rods, and spirals.

twist.text: Bacteria reproduce by splitting in two. Under ideal conditions, one becomes a billion in 10 hours.

climax.text: There are more bacteria in your gut than human cells in your entire body.

punchline.text: Simple, ancient, and everywhere you look.
